name: task-handler
summary: Worker that executes a queued task, receiving its typed payload and running the actual work.
description: Creates a task handler and registers it in an initializer
problem: You've defined a task that gets queued, and now you need the worker that actually does the work — receiving the typed task payload, running side effects, and surfacing results or failures so the queue can retry intelligently.
appliesWhen: A task class already exists (created via phpnomad/task)
You're ready to define what happens when the task runs
You want the worker logic isolated from the dispatcher
avoidWhen: The task class doesn't exist yet — create it first via phpnomad/task
The work is synchronous and request-bound — don't use a task pipeline
You're handling many similar tasks with shared logic — consider one handler per task type still, but extract shared logic into a service
examples: Add a handler for the SendWelcomeEmail task
I need a worker that processes the SyncOrderToWarehouse task
Create the handler that runs the GenerateMonthlyReport task
tradeoffs: Pairing of task + handler doubles the file count for an async unit of work. The split keeps payload separate from execution, which makes both halves easier to test.
outputs: A {name} task handler class implementing the appropriate handler interface
A registration mapping the task class to this handler in the initializer
postApply: Implement the handler's handle() method with the actual worker logic
Decide how failures should signal retry-vs-give-up to the queue

task.description: FQCN of the task class to handle
task.aiHint: Use the FQCN of an existing task class (created via phpnomad/task).

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register in
initializer.aiHint: Pick the initializer that owns related task handlers for this domain.
